notes: OpenCode `/new` reset supersession — RECORDED 2026-05-26 (driver_capability now ready). OpenCode's Source is ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite, core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go): a transient proc-<PID> pre-session is born ready at launch, hands off to a SQLite-keyed ses_… session on the first prompt, runs one turn, then a TUI /new mints a SECOND ses_… under the same PID with a fresh session_id and no turn-1 history; the daemon RETIRES the old row (transcript_removed for the first ses_…, the same-PID cleanup — opencode emits this; codex/pi /clear does not) and a second prompt drives a turn on the new row. Validated 7/7 phases against this recording. The reset is delivered by drive-opencode-interactive.sh's live-TUI path (launch opencode TUI under tmux, tmux send-keys, a reset_session step, multi-session session.uuids/transcript.paths); headless `opencode run` CANNOT deliver in-REPL slash commands (PROBED 2026-05-26: it stored `/new` as user text in ONE ses_…), which is why the live-TUI path exists.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 2s of opencode launch
After the first prompt lands, the SQLite-keyed ses_… session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh session_id
First user prompt drives ready → working on the ses_… session
First turn completes back to ready (same ses_… session id)

Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — after /new, a fresh ses_… session_id never seen before appears in ready; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'the conversation was reset'
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
